title: erigeron acris subsp. brachycephalus
intro: Erigeron acris subsp. brachycephalus is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, known for its small, white or pinkish flowers and ability to thrive in dry, rocky areas. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which are thought to aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. The health benefits of Erigeron acris subsp. brachycephalus are said to include reducing fever, promoting digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and salves, have been used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
